expectations.text: A0: Agent produced both output files with real content
expectations.evidence: quality-scenarios.md (232 lines, 15 scenarios) and analysis.md (130 lines, full rationale) both present with substantive content.

expectations.text: A1: Scenarios use ATAM format (Source, Stimulus, Environment, Artifact, Response, Response Measure)
expectations.evidence: All 15 scenarios (QS-001 through QS-015) have all 6 ATAM fields: Source, Stimulus, Environment, Artifact, Response, Response Measure.

expectations.text: A2: Diverse test types — at least 3 different types, not all fitness-functions
expectations.evidence: 5 different test types used: unit-test (1), integration-test (8), load-test (3), chaos-test (2), fitness-function (1).

expectations.text: A3: Style fitness functions NOT duplicated — no scenarios for structural invariants already covered by style FFs
expectations.evidence: Style FFs cover: service boundary alignment, service count 4-12, no service-to-service chatter <5 calls, database sharing discipline. No scenario duplicates these. QS-007 tests payment data isolation (security concern), not the general DB sharing discipline check. QS-012 tests module isolation testability (can tests run independently), not service boundary alignment.

expectations.text: A4: Tradeoffs identified — at least 1 explicit tension/conflict between characteristics
expectations.evidence: 3 tradeoffs documented: Performance vs Security (PCI logging vs checkout latency), Availability vs Data Consistency (uptime vs stale data), Scalability vs Cost (pre-provisioned vs auto-scaling). Plus 2 sensitivity points.

expectations.text: A5: Summary table present — table listing all scenarios with test types
expectations.evidence: Two tables present: 'Scenario Summary' table with ID, Characteristic, Scenario, Test Type, Priority columns; and 'Test Type Distribution' table with counts and scenario IDs.

expectations.text: A6: Response measures are CONCRETE — numbers, thresholds, or specific observable outcomes
expectations.evidence: All measures are concrete: 'p95 < 100ms at 500 concurrent users' (QS-001), 'failover within 30 seconds; zero data loss' (QS-005), '100% of unauthenticated requests return 401' (QS-008), 'error rate stays below 1%' (QS-010), etc.
